name: TOMI Environmental Solutions, Inc.
business: TOMI Environmental Solutions, Inc. provides disinfection and decontamination solutions using its SteraMist product line based on Binary Ionization Technology (BIT), which produces ionized hydrogen peroxide. It serves four primary market divisions: Life Sciences, Hospital-Healthcare, Food Safety, and Commercial.
namedCustomers: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC)
United States Army Medical Research Institute of Infectious Diseases (USAMRIID)
United States Army Medical Research Institute of Chemical Defense (USAMRICD)

exposures.sentence: On March 20, 2025, we deployed SteraMist iHP technology at NASA s Johnson Space Center, marking our expansion into the aerospace sector.
Our current client roster includes ... United States Army Medical Research Institute of Infectious Diseases and of Chemical Defense ( USAMRIID and USAMRICD ).
Our development goals include the development and commercialization of a variety of sanitizing robotic devices and backpack units.

the heart monitoring device project is nearing completion and is scheduled for Factory Acceptance Testing ("FAT") in the near term. This initiative... reflects our ability to design and deploy iHP technology within highly regulated medical device environments
SteraMist demonstrated efficacy in combating Honeybee Colony Collapse, positioning TOMI to support global food security through environmental biosecurity.

Our current client roster includes Fortune 100 pharmaceutical and healthcare companies such as Pfizer, Merck, AbbVie, Bausch & Lomb, ThermoFisher Scientific, Eli Lilly and Company, FUJIFILM Diosynth Biotechnologies USA, and Fresenius Kabi

Custom Engineered System (CES) is a permanent installation for rooms requiring routine automated decontamination, fully integrated into the facility s HVAC system with permanently mounted applicators and optional SCADA monitoring.

catalysts.event: Signed purchase order for iHP integration in sterile manufacturing passthrough fill boxes valued at $500,000
catalysts.sentence: On December 18, 2025, we secured a signed purchase order valued at approximately $500,000 from a global biopharmaceutical leader for the integration of SteraMist iHP into passthrough fill boxes used in sterile manufacturing.

catalysts.event: Anticipated award of remaining integration pipeline in first half of 2026
catalysts.sentence: We anticipate the remaining pipeline to be awarded in the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: First draw on ELOC generating gross proceeds of $94,130
catalysts.sentence: In February 2026, we made our first draw generating gross proceeds of $94,130.
